summary:
Complete data inventory across both projects. NewStrategyFable5/cache (4.6 GB) holds four 1-minute bid/ask bar sets: bars_1m_ic (IC Markets native feed, 37 instruments, <SYM>_IC_1m.parquet, broker server time UTC+2/+3 tz-naive, 2020-01-02 -> 2025-12-31, 9 cols bid/ask OHLC + n_ticks), bars_1m (Dukascopy 2nd feed, 14 instruments, <SYM>_2020_2025_1m.parquet, true UTC tz-naive, 11 cols adding volume + spread_mean, index 'ts'), bars_1m_ext (10 instruments, <SYM>_2015_2025_1m.parquet, Duka schema, 2015-2025 — NOT mentioned in cache/README.md), and bars_1m_holdout which — contradicting cache/README.md — actually contains 2026H1 forward data (<SYM>_2026H1_1m.parquet, 14 syms, Duka schema UTC, Jan 1-Apr 30 2026; EURUSD and XAUUSD extend to May 31 2026).

FableMultiAssets2 derives all its hourly caches from NSF5 data (README line 131: price data read-only from NSF5 cache/bars_1m_ic). research_cache/ = 37 syms hourly mid-OHLC (o,h,l,c,spread,n_ticks,n_min,rel_spread), 2020-01-02 00:00 -> 2025-12-31 23:00, built by research/prep_data.py, plus audit.json. research_cache/exec/ = 37 syms execution-grade hourly bars (bid_o,ask_o,bid_c,ask_c,bid_l,ask_h,spread — first-tick opens, last-tick closes, intrabar worst extremes) built by research/build_exec_bars.py for the single-account engine. research_cache_duka/ = 14 syms Duka 2020-2025 converted UTC->America/New_York+7h to server time. research_cache_ext/ = 34 syms 2015-2020 with ASSIGNED per-class typical spreads (edge-persistence quality, not worst-mark), built by research/build_cache_2015.py from data_dl/ (22 syms h1 bid OHLC CSVs, ms-epoch UTC) plus /Users/dsalamanca/vs_env/data/extended (12 syms M1 Duka). research_cache_fwd/ = 14 syms, IC 2020-2025 server-time concatenated with converted 2026H1 holdout, ending 2026-05-01 02:00 (EURUSD/XAUUSD 2026-06-01 02:00) — gives sleeves real warmup into the fresh 2026 window.

A shared /Users/dsalamanca/vs_env/data/ dir (~35 GB, outside both projects) holds the upstream raw material: yearly/ 33 GB Dukascopy ticks 2020-2025, 2026_ytd/ 1.8 GB raw 2026 downloads, extended/ 445 MB 2015-2020 M1, external/ VIX CSVs, and a DO_NOT_USE.md documenting retracted mixed-timezone tick files (Duka UTC + IC UTC+2 concatenated unnormalized) that produced a retracted PF 1.49 lock — the canonical timezone landmine for any merge work. Environment check: python 3.13.12 with pandas 2.3.3, numpy 2.4.2, numba 0.64.0 (njit smoke test compiles and runs), pyarrow 23.0.1. Copy cost into FableMultiAssets3 (currently empty): NSF5 cache 4.6 GB + FMA2 caches/data_dl ~246 MB; whole projects 6.0 GB + 847 MB; 138 GiB free disk. 2026 forward data exists ONLY in NSF5 bars_1m_holdout, FMA2 research_cache_fwd, and shared data/2026_ytd — everything else ends 2025-12-31 (ext caches end 2020-12-31).
key_facts: NSF5 bars_1m_ic: 37 instruments = FX21 (AUDCAD AUDJPY AUDNZD AUDUSD CADCHF CADJPY EURCAD EURCHF EURGBP EURJPY EURNOK EURNZD EURSEK EURUSD GBPJPY GBPUSD NZDCAD NZDJPY NZDUSD USDCHF USDJPY) + crypto4 (BTCUSD ETHUSD SOLUSD XRPUSD) + indices6 (DAX JP225 UK100 US30 USA500 USTEC) + commodities6 (XAGUSD XAUUSD XBRUSD XNGUSD XPTUSD XTIUSD); 2.1 GB

IC 1m schema: bid_o/bid_h/bid_l/bid_c/ask_o/ask_h/ask_l/ask_c float64 + n_ticks int64; unnamed tz-naive DatetimeIndex in broker SERVER time (daily break at hour 0 = 17:00 ET); EURUSD 2,234,871 rows 2020-01-02 00:00 -> 2025-12-31 23:58
IC feed exceptions: SOLUSD starts 2022-03-14; DAX ends 2025-12-30 23:58; UK100 ends 2025-12-31 14:49; indices start 03:00-08:00 on day 1

NSF5 bars_1m (Dukascopy 2nd feed): 14 syms <SYM>_2020_2025_1m.parquet; schema adds n_ticks int32, volume float32, spread_mean float64; index 'ts' tz-naive TRUE UTC (EURUSD 2020-01-01 22:01 -> 2025-12-31 21:58); 1.1 GB; the name does NOT say duka — no _IC suffix means Duka (cache/README.md:17)

NSF5 bars_1m_ext: 10 syms <SYM>_2015_2025_1m.parquet, Duka schema, 2015-01-01 22:00 -> 2025-12-31 (BTCUSD from 2017-05-07, ETHUSD from 2017-12-11); EURUSD 4,097,249 rows; 1.2 GB; ABSENT from cache/README.md

NSF5 bars_1m_holdout ACTUALLY contains <SYM>_2026H1_1m.parquet (14 syms, Duka schema, UTC): 2026-01-01 -> 2026-04-30 for 12 syms; EURUSD (150,414 rows) and XAUUSD (142,074 rows) extend to 2026-05-31; 71 MB — cache/README.md:11 wrongly says 'Holdout split of the Duka feed, <SYM>_2020_2025_1m.parquet'

IC ticker rename map (cache/README.md): DE40->DAX, US500->USA500, USTEC = IC's NASDAQ-100 (not NAS100); raw MT5 IC_*_m1.csv exports (~5 GB) deleted after build_ic_feed.py conversion — re-export from MT5 if needed

FMA2 research_cache: 37 syms <SYM>_1h.parquet; schema o,h,l,c (mid OHLC), spread (mean ask_c-bid_c), n_ticks, n_min (minutes present, data-quality), rel_spread; 2020-01-02 00:00 -> 2025-12-31 23:00; EURUSD 37,348 rows; built by research/prep_data.py from NSF5 bars_1m_ic ('the permitted data path'); includes audit.json with per-symbol n_1m/n_1h/first/last/rel_spread_med/p90/px endpoints

FMA2 research_cache/exec: 37 syms <SYM>_1h_exec.parquet; schema bid_o,ask_o (first-tick, fills at next bar open), bid_c,ask_c (close marks), bid_l,ask_h (intrabar worst extremes for DD), spread; built by research/build_exec_bars.py; docstring: hourly bars with true intrabar extremes reproduce 1m execution for notional no-stop sleeves within negligible tolerance (~3M rows -> ~37k)

FMA2 research_cache_duka: 14 syms 1h, Dukascopy 2020-2025 converted UTC -> America/New_York -> +7h (DST-correct, break lands at hour 0) by research/build_ext_cache.py; ends 2025-12-31 23:00 server time

FMA2 research_cache_ext: 34 syms (37 minus SOLUSD, XRPUSD, XPTUSD) 2015-01-02 -> 2020-12-31 (BTC from 2017-05-08, ETH from 2017-12-12); index name 'timestamp'; spreads ASSIGNED per class (FX major 4e-5, FX cross 1.5e-4, scandi 3e-3, XAUUSD 2.3e-5, DAX 7.7e-4, etc. per build_cache_2015.py REL_SPREAD dict) — single-price sources, NOT worst-mark execution grade

FMA2 research_cache_fwd: 14 syms = IC 2020-2025 server-time + converted 2026H1 holdout CONCATENATED (build_ext_cache.py docstring: gives sleeves real warmup into fresh 2026 window); most end 2026-05-01 02:00, EURUSD/XAUUSD end 2026-06-01 02:00; EURUSD has 2,516 hourly rows in 2026

FMA2 data_dl: 132 CSVs = 22 instruments x 6 years (2015-2020): AUDCAD AUDJPY AUDNZD CADCHF CADJPY DAX EURCAD EURCHF EURNOK EURNZD EURSEK GBPJPY JP225 NZDCAD NZDJPY UK100 US30 USDCHF XAGUSD XBRUSD XNGUSD XTIUSD; format: header 'timestamp,open,high,low,close', ms-epoch UTC timestamps, h1 bid OHLC, ~5,850-6,230 rows/year; 34 MB
Timezone conversion rule used throughout FMA2: to_server(): UTC -> tz_convert('America/New_York') + 7h -> tz_localize(None); defined in both build_ext_cache.py and build_cache_2015.py

Shared /Users/dsalamanca/vs_env/data/: yearly/ 33 GB (105 files <SYM>_<YEAR>_DUKASCOPY.parquet tick data 2020-2025), 2026_ytd/ 1.8 GB (14 syms <SYM>_2026-01-01_2026-04-30_DUKASCOPY.parquet; EURUSD/XAUUSD to 2026-05-31 — the raw source of the 2026H1 holdout), extended/ 445 MB (12 syms x 2015-2020 M1: AUDUSD BTCUSD ETHUSD EURGBP EURJPY EURUSD GBPUSD NZDUSD US500 USDJPY USTEC XAUUSD), external/ VIX_History.csv VIX3M_History.csv VXX_daily_stooq.csv vx_contracts/
Environment: python 3.13.12 (conda-forge), pandas 2.3.3, numpy 2.4.2, numba 0.64.0 (njit compile+run smoke test PASSED), pyarrow 23.0.1

Disk: NSF5 project 6.0 GB (cache 4.6 GB: ic 2.1G, duka 1.1G, ext 1.2G, holdout 71M, walkforward_v9.parquet 66M); FMA2 project 847 MB (research_cache 117M incl exec 54M, duka 29M, ext 41M, fwd 25M, data_dl 34M); FableMultiAssets3 currently empty (only .claude/); 138 GiB free on disk

2026 forward data locations (ONLY these): NSF5 cache/bars_1m_holdout (1m, UTC, Duka format), FMA2 research_cache_fwd (1h, server time, appended to 2020-2025 history), shared data/2026_ytd (raw ticks). bars_1m_ic, bars_1m, bars_1m_ext, research_cache, research_cache_duka, exec all end 2025-12-31; research_cache_ext ends 2020-12-31

FMA2 README.md:131: 'Price data: read-only from /Users/dsalamanca/vs_env/NewStrategyFable5/cache/bars_1m_ic' — FMA2 references NSF5's cache in place rather than copying; builder scripts hardcode the absolute NSF5 paths
architecture: Data layer architecture: NSF5 owns the 1-minute bid/ask ground truth in cache/ (two independent feeds for worst-feed cross-validation via mt5/reconcile/multifeed_optim.py — IC Markets native in bars_1m_ic/ with _IC_1m suffix in broker server time, Dukascopy in bars_1m/ with _2020_2025_1m suffix in true UTC; plus bars_1m_ext/ 2015-2025 Duka and bars_1m_holdout/ 2026H1 Duka forward data). FMA2 is purely a downstream consumer: four builder scripts in research/ (prep_data.py, build_exec_bars.py, build_ext_cache.py, build_cache_2015.py) read NSF5's 1m caches via hardcoded absolute paths and emit hourly server-time-aligned parquets into research_cache{,_duka,_ext,_fwd}. Two hourly bar grades exist: research bars (mid OHLC + rel_spread, for signal research) and exec bars (bid/ask opens/closes + intrabar worst extremes, for the single-account execution engine). All FMA2 caches share one timestamp convention: tz-naive broker server time with daily break at hour 0 = 17:00 ET, produced by the verified UTC->America/New_York+7h conversion. Upstream of everything is the shared /Users/dsalamanca/vs_env/data/ tree (33 GB Duka ticks by year, 2026 YTD ticks, 2015-2020 M1 extended set, VIX externals).
how_to_run: Rebuild FMA2 hourly research cache + audit.json (37 syms from NSF5 IC 1m): cd /Users/dsalamanca/vs_env/FableMultiAssets2 && python3 research/prep_data.py  -> writes research_cache/<SYM>_1h.parquet + research_cache/audit.json
Rebuild execution-grade hourly bars (37 syms): cd /Users/dsalamanca/vs_env/FableMultiAssets2 && python3 research/build_exec_bars.py  -> writes research_cache/exec/<SYM>_1h_exec.parquet
Rebuild Duka 2nd-feed + 2026-forward caches (14 syms): cd /Users/dsalamanca/vs_env/FableMultiAssets2 && python3 research/build_ext_cache.py  -> writes research_cache_duka/ and research_cache_fwd/
Rebuild 2015-2020 pre-period OOS cache (34 syms, parked): cd /Users/dsalamanca/vs_env/FableMultiAssets2 && python3 research/build_cache_2015.py  -> writes research_cache_ext/

Quick parquet sanity check: python3 -c "import pandas as pd; df=pd.read_parquet('/Users/dsalamanca/vs_env/NewStrategyFable5/cache/bars_1m_ic/EURUSD_IC_1m.parquet'); print(df.shape, list(df.columns), df.index.min(), df.index.max())"  -> expect (2234871, 9), bid/ask OHLC + n_ticks, 2020-01-02 00:00 -> 2025-12-31 23:58

Environment check (passes today): python3 -c "import pandas, numpy, numba, pyarrow; print(pandas.__version__, numpy.__version__, numba.__version__, pyarrow.__version__)"  -> 2.3.3 2.4.2 0.64.0 23.0.1 on python 3.13.12
official_numbers: None encountered in this data-inventory scope — no engine performance numbers live in the cache directories themselves. The only performance figure seen anywhere in the scanned data docs is in /Users/dsalamanca/vs_env/data/DO_NOT_USE.md: the retracted eurgbp_modern_v3_4 / eurgbp_modern_ext_v3_4 locks (PF 1.49, CI [1.094, 1.980]), explicitly RETRACTED 2026-05-04 as a mixed-timezone data artifact (Duka UTC concatenated with IC UTC+2 unnormalized); both timezone-consistent hybrid rebuilds fail the v3.4 CPCV alpha gate. Data-quality reference numbers: FMA2 research_cache/audit.json holds per-symbol row counts, date ranges, and median/p90 relative spreads (e.g. AUDCAD n_1m=2,235,293, n_1h=37,348, rel_spread_med=2.39e-5) for the 37 IC instruments.
pitfalls: STALE DOC: /Users/dsalamanca/vs_env/NewStrategyFable5/cache/README.md (line 11) says bars_1m_holdout = 'Holdout split of the Duka feed' with <SYM>_2020_2025_1m.parquet names; the directory actually contains <SYM>_2026H1_1m.parquet 2026 forward data. The README also omits bars_1m_ext/ entirely (dir modified Jul 9, README Jul 6).

TIMEZONE LANDMINE (the project's canonical failure mode): IC feed is tz-naive broker server time (UTC+2/+3); Dukascopy files are tz-naive TRUE UTC. Mixing without the verified to_server() conversion (UTC -> America/New_York + 7h) reproduces the retracted PF-1.49 artifact documented in /Users/dsalamanca/vs_env/data/DO_NOT_USE.md — session filters slice different real market windows per year and manufacture fake alpha that passes CPCV.

prep_data.py docstring says research_cache index is 'tz-naive UTC' but it inherits IC broker server time unchanged; build_ext_cache.py correctly calls research_cache the 'existing server-time cache'. Trust build_ext_cache.py.

Duka naming trap: files with NO feed tag (_2020_2025_1m) ARE the Dukascopy feed. Rename to bars_1m_duka/<SYM>_DUKA_1m.parquet is a deferred TODO blocked on hardcoded paths in multifeed_optim.py, scripts/build_bars.py, engine/backtest.py (cache/README.md:40-44).

research_cache_ext (2015-2020) uses ASSIGNED per-asset-class typical spreads on single-price feeds — fine for edge-persistence tests, NOT valid for worst-mark execution accounting; build_cache_2015.py says so explicitly and FMA2 README marks it 'parked'.
2026 data (bars_1m_holdout, research_cache_fwd tail, data/2026_ytd) is holdout/forward-validation data — do not let it leak into training/optimization in the new FableMultiAssets3 book.
Uneven forward windows: 12 syms have 2026 data through Apr 30 only; EURUSD and XAUUSD through May 31 — cross-symbol forward comparisons after 2026-05-01 are 2-symbol only.

Symbol coverage gaps: SOLUSD starts 2022-03-14 (all caches); BTCUSD/ETHUSD ext history starts 2017-05/2017-12; research_cache_ext lacks SOLUSD, XRPUSD, XPTUSD; DAX ends 2025-12-30 and UK100 ends 2025-12-31 14:00 in the 2020-2025 caches.

FMA2 builder scripts hardcode absolute paths into NewStrategyFable5 — a copied/merged FableMultiAssets3 must either replicate NSF5/cache at the same path, copy the 4.6 GB, or patch SRC/IC_1M/HOLD_1M/DUKA_1M constants in prep_data.py, build_exec_bars.py, build_ext_cache.py.
Do NOT blind-copy /Users/dsalamanca/vs_env/data/yearly (33 GB ticks) — the projects only need the derived 1m/1h caches; raw IC MT5 CSVs are already deleted (~5 GB, re-exportable from MT5).
data_dl CSVs are BID OHLC (not mid) with ms-epoch UTC timestamps and no spread column — spreads must be synthesized as build_cache_2015.py does.

FMA2 exec bars exist only for the 2020-2025 window (built from bars_1m_ic); there are NO execution-grade bars for the 2015-2020 ext period or the 2026 forward window in FMA2 — fwd-window execution tests currently rely on research-grade mid bars.
